styles.name: Modern Farmhouse
signature: black metal and matte-black hardware accents

materials.item: white oak or hickory wide-plank flooring
honed white or gray quartz counters

materials.note: true steel costly; aluminum look-alikes cut cost

materials.item: handmade white subway or zellige-look tile

styles.lighting: Abundant daylight through black-gridded windows, plus black metal pendants, lantern fixtures, and rubbed-bronze or matte-black sconces.
styles.furniture: Slipcovered linen sofas, farm tables, spindle and windsor chairs, woven jute rugs, and worn-leather accent seating in relaxed, family-friendly forms.
styles.interiorPrompt: modern farmhouse style with white shiplap walls, black steel-framed windows, warm wide-plank wood floors, shaker cabinetry and matte-black fixtures
styles.exteriorPrompt: modern farmhouse exterior with white board-and-batten siding, black-framed windows, a standing-seam metal porch roof, natural wood accents and a gravel-and-native-planting yard
styles.designerNotes: A mid-tier, high-resale-value look that suits SoCal ranch and tract homes well and reads clean without feeling cold. The most common mistake is going too gray and rustic; keep whites warm and let one or two real wood and black-metal moments carry it. Steel windows are the budget line item, so aluminum look-alikes keep it attainable.

styles.name: Japandi
signature: warm neutral limewashed or plaster walls
clean uninterrupted sightlines and negative space
black-stained wood or charred (shou sugi ban) accents
linen and wool textiles in muted tones

materials.item: white oak or ash flooring, matte finish

materials.note: white oak or walnut slats; custom millwork

materials.item: shou sugi ban charred cedar accent

styles.lighting: Soft, diffused daylight and warm 2700K glow from paper lanterns, linear wood pendants, and hidden LED cove lighting; nothing harsh or bright-white.
styles.furniture: Low, light-framed pieces blending Scandinavian function with Japanese craft: platform beds, spindle and wishbone chairs, floor cushions, and pieces that leave the floor visible.
styles.interiorPrompt: japandi style with warm limewashed plaster walls, low natural white-oak furniture, a vertical wood slat feature, handmade ceramics and soft diffused lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: japandi exterior blending pale plaster and warm wood with charred-cedar accents, a low horizontal roofline, gravel and moss courtyard and minimalist black-framed openings
styles.designerNotes: A calm, elevated look that photographs beautifully and fits SoCal's indoor-outdoor living; costs sit mid-to-upper because the craft finishes (plaster, slat walls, real ceramics) carry the room. The frequent miss is treating it as gray minimalism; Japandi must feel warm and handmade. Editing is the skill here, empty space is a feature, not a gap to fill.

styles.name: Japanese Zen
signature: restrained natural materials and empty space (ma)

materials.item: tatami mat flooring (rush over rice straw)
shoji screens, cedar frame + washi paper
paper lantern and andon floor lighting

styles.lighting: Filtered, screen-diffused daylight and low warm ambient light from paper lanterns and floor andon; deliberately dim, shadow-appreciating (in-ei) rather than bright.
styles.furniture: Minimal, low, floor-oriented: futons, zabuton cushions, low tables, a single tokonoma display; furniture is sparse and every object is intentional.
styles.interiorPrompt: traditional Japanese Zen style with shoji paper screens, tatami mat flooring, exposed cedar post-and-beam structure, a tokonoma alcove and soft filtered light
styles.exteriorPrompt: traditional Japanese exterior with deep tiled eaves, exposed timber structure, shoji-lit openings, an engawa veranda and a raked-gravel and moss garden with stone basin
styles.designerNotes: A demanding, craft-heavy style rarely done full-scale in SoCal remodels; more often clients want a Zen-informed room (a shoji-screened den, a tatami tea corner). True tatami and shoji are import/specialty costs with real lead times, so budget accordingly or use Japanese-inspired detailing. The soul of it is restraint and shadow, so resist over-decorating and keep the palette to natural material tones.

styles.name: Scandinavian
signature: minimal restrained decor with a few graphic pieces

materials.item: pale oak, ash, or birch flooring
flat white or soft-white painted walls

styles.lighting: Every trick to amplify scarce light: white surfaces, sheer or bare windows, plus warm layered lamps, opal-glass pendants, and abundant candles for evening hygge.
styles.furniture: Iconic mid-century-adjacent Nordic pieces, light wood legs, tapered forms, functional storage, softened by sheepskins, wool, and linen.
styles.interiorPrompt: scandinavian style with bright white walls, pale blond-wood floors, clean functional furniture, layered wool textiles and a few black graphic accents
styles.exteriorPrompt: scandinavian exterior with simple gabled forms, white or pale-wood cladding, black-framed windows, uncluttered lines and a minimal birch-and-gravel yard
styles.designerNotes: A budget-friendly, light-forward style that works well in smaller SoCal homes and rentals because white walls and pale wood make spaces feel larger. The mistake is letting it read cold and sterile; hygge textiles and warm 2700K light are what make it livable. It overlaps heavily with Japandi and minimalism, so the differentiator is cozy, light, and a touch of playful graphic contrast.

styles.name: Mid-Century Modern
styles.lighting: Generous glass floods rooms with daylight; at night, sculptural statements (Sputnik, Nelson bubble, arc lamps) plus warm globe pendants define the era.
styles.furniture: Iconic low-slung pieces (Eames, Wegner, credenzas on tapered legs), walnut casegoods, tweed and leather upholstery, and organic-shaped coffee tables.
styles.interiorPrompt: mid-century modern style with walnut wood tones, clean low horizontal lines, an exposed beam ceiling, floor-to-ceiling glass, tapered-leg furniture and sculptural lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: mid-century modern exterior with a low-slope or butterfly roof, post-and-beam framing, floor-to-ceiling glass, breeze-block screen and clean geometric landscaping
styles.designerNotes: Deeply native to SoCal (Eichler tracts, Palm Springs, LA hillside homes), so it often means restoring what a house already wants to be, which keeps costs reasonable if bones survive. The classic error is mixing in heavy traditional or gray-modern pieces that flatten the warmth; commit to walnut, warm accent color, and honest structure. Original post-and-beam ceilings and clerestory glass are worth preserving over replacing.

styles.name: Spanish Revival
signature: arched doorways, windows, and wall niches
red clay barrel-tile roof and terracotta floors

materials.item: smooth or troweled lime plaster walls

materials.note: custom iron shop; Emtek iron sets

styles.lighting: Deep-set arched windows cast warm shadow-play; fixtures are wrought-iron chandeliers, mica or amber-glass lanterns, and hand-punched tin sconces.
styles.furniture: Heavy carved dark-wood pieces, leather and iron accents, terracotta and talavera pottery, woven textiles, and Spanish-colonial silhouettes.
styles.interiorPrompt: Spanish Revival style with warm white plaster walls, arched openings, saltillo terracotta floors, hand-painted tile accents, dark wood beams and wrought-iron fixtures
styles.exteriorPrompt: Spanish Revival exterior with white stucco walls, a red clay barrel-tile roof, arched entry, wrought-iron window grilles, a tiled courtyard fountain and drought-tolerant Mediterranean planting
styles.designerNotes: One of SoCal's defining vernaculars (Santa Barbara, LA, the entire 1920s-30s housing stock), so leaning into it usually respects the home and helps resale. Costs run mid-to-upper because hand-painted tile, real plaster, iron, and clay roofing are craft trades, but the payoff is authenticity. The biggest tell of a fake is machine-perfect, orange-toned tile and flat drywall; insist on hand-troweled plaster and real (or convincingly sealed) saltillo.

styles.name: Mediterranean
signature: light cream and sandy plaster walls
styles.lighting: Sun-washed daylight through arches; lantern pendants, iron chandeliers, and brass sconces give a warm golden evening glow.
styles.furniture: Relaxed but elegant: carved wood, rush and cane seating, slipcovered linen, wrought-iron tables, and terracotta pottery, less heavy than Spanish Colonial.
styles.interiorPrompt: Mediterranean style with warm cream plaster walls, arched openings, travertine floors, terracotta and ceramic tile accents, wood beams and wrought-iron fixtures
styles.exteriorPrompt: Mediterranean villa exterior with cream stucco, a low terracotta-tile roof, arched loggia, stone accents, a courtyard fountain and olive-and-cypress landscaping
styles.designerNotes: Reads lighter and sunnier than Spanish Colonial, so it suits SoCal clients who want warmth without heaviness; it shares arches and plaster but leans cream, brass, and blue rather than dark wood and iron. Mid-to-upper cost driven by stone flooring and plaster. The common failure is the dated 2000s 'Tuscan' brown sponge-paint version; keep walls smooth and pale and let stone, iron, and one accent color do the work.

styles.name: Tuscan
signature: heavy carved wood and iron furniture
stone or brick accent walls and arches

materials.item: warm troweled plaster or Roman clay walls
carved-stone or brick range hood / arch

styles.lighting: Warm, low, golden light: iron chandeliers, candle-style sconces, and under-cabinet warmth; deliberately cozy and dim rather than bright.
styles.furniture: Substantial carved dark-wood tables and armoires, leather and tapestry upholstery, wrought-iron accents, and rustic pottery and copper.
styles.interiorPrompt: Tuscan style with warm earth-toned plaster walls, rough-hewn timber beams, terracotta cotto floors, carved dark-wood furniture and wrought-iron accents
styles.exteriorPrompt: Tuscan villa exterior with golden ochre stucco, a weathered terracotta roof, stone accents, arched openings, a pergola with grapevines and cypress trees
styles.designerNotes: The most misused style of the 2000s remodel boom, so many SoCal clients now read heavy 'Tuscan brown' as dated; steer toward its refined Old-World roots (real plaster, stone, aged iron) rather than the sponge-paint cliche. Costs are mid-tier but the timber and stone can climb. If a client loves the warmth but not the heaviness, nudge them toward the lighter Mediterranean palette while keeping the terracotta and beams.

styles.name: Coastal (Hamptons)
signature: navy, soft blue, and white palette

materials.item: wide-plank white or driftwood-toned oak floors
handmade white and blue ceramic tile

styles.lighting: Bright and airy: white surfaces bounce daylight, layered with polished-nickel lanterns, glass-and-brass pendants, and woven-shade fixtures.
styles.furniture: Comfortable coastal-traditional: slipcovered sofas, cane and rattan accents, weathered-wood tables, woven baskets, and blue-and-white porcelain.
styles.interiorPrompt: coastal Hamptons style with crisp white shiplap walls, navy and blue accents, wide-plank light oak floors, coffered ceilings, slipcovered furniture and polished-nickel fixtures
styles.exteriorPrompt: Hamptons coastal exterior with white or gray shingle siding, white trim, gabled rooflines, a covered porch, hydrangea beds and a manicured lawn
styles.designerNotes: A crowd-pleasing, high-resale style that suits SoCal beach communities and buyers who want polished rather than rustic; it's dressier than California Casual and cooler than farmhouse. Mid-to-upper cost from millwork (coffers, paneling) and marble. The trap is theme-park nautical decor; keep it architectural (paneling, molding, navy-and-white) and let one or two coastal cues suffice.

styles.name: California Casual
signature: warm white walls and light oak
big glass sliders and clerestory light

materials.item: warm-white plaster or flat paint walls

styles.lighting: Sun-drenched daylight through big glass, layered with organic linen-shade pendants, rattan fixtures, and warm dimmable ambient light.
styles.furniture: Low, comfortable, natural: slipcovered and boucle seating, oak and travertine tables, woven poufs, and abundant potted greenery.
styles.interiorPrompt: California casual style with warm white walls, light oak floors, big glass sliders, natural jute and rattan textures, linen furniture and abundant plants
styles.exteriorPrompt: California casual exterior with white stucco or board siding, warm-wood accents, large glass sliders opening to a patio, drought-tolerant natives and a relaxed indoor-outdoor yard
styles.designerNotes: Arguably the default aspirational look for SoCal right now: warm, breezy, plant-filled, and built around indoor-outdoor living, which suits the climate and housing stock perfectly. It's forgiving and mid-tier because much of it is paint, oak, texture, and greenery rather than heavy materials. The key is warmth and layered natural texture; if it drifts gray and hard it becomes generic modern, so keep the oat-and-oak palette and organic forms.

styles.name: Contemporary Luxe
signature: clean architectural lines and hidden detailing

materials.item: bookmatched marble / porcelain slab feature walls
brushed brass, bronze, or blackened-steel trim
integrated linear LED and cove lighting

styles.lighting: Layered and architectural: hidden LED cove and toe-kick light, statement sculptural chandeliers, backlit stone, and dimmable warm ambient washing.
styles.furniture: Sleek Italian-modern pieces, low profiles, plush velvet and leather, polished-metal bases, and one or two sculptural statement objects.
styles.interiorPrompt: contemporary luxe style with bookmatched stone slab walls, high-contrast neutral palette, clean architectural lines, brass accents, integrated LED lighting and plush velvet furniture
styles.exteriorPrompt: contemporary luxe exterior with clean stucco-and-stone volumes, floor-to-ceiling glass, a flat roof, dramatic linear landscape lighting and sculptural minimalist planting
styles.designerNotes: The top-of-market look for SoCal new builds and hillside remodels; it's the most expensive tier here because it lives or dies on real stone slabs, integrated lighting, and precise millwork with no forgiving rustic texture. The mistake is 'builder glam', cheap gloss and too much shiny metal, which reads tacky; luxe is restraint plus a few genuinely expensive materials done flawlessly. Warm the LEDs and keep the palette disciplined.

styles.name: Minimalist
signature: clean uninterrupted surfaces and hidden storage

materials.item: matte white or greige flat paint

styles.lighting: Clean and unobtrusive: recessed and linear LED, hidden sources, and daylight treated as a material; fixtures disappear rather than decorate.
styles.furniture: Very few pieces, each sculptural and low: a clean-lined sofa, one statement chair, a simple table; storage is concealed and surfaces stay clear.
styles.interiorPrompt: minimalist style with a monochromatic neutral palette, seamless plaster surfaces, handleless flat cabinetry, generous negative space and concealed lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: minimalist exterior with clean monolithic stucco volumes, flush detailing, frameless glass, a flat roof and sparse architectural planting
avoid: visible clutter and open shelving overload
cheap surfaces that show every flaw
styles.designerNotes: Deceptively expensive to execute well because minimalism hides nothing; seamless plaster, handleless cabinets, and shadow-gap reveals demand precise, high-skill trades and generous storage design. It suits SoCal clients who want serenity and have the discipline to live clutter-free. Warn clients that 'simple' does not mean 'cheap', the labor and tolerances are the cost, and a single visible flaw undermines the whole look.

styles.name: Industrial Loft

materials.item: black steel-frame windows and glass partitions

styles.lighting: High-contrast and utilitarian: big factory windows by day, exposed Edison bulbs, cage pendants, and track lighting for a warm-but-raw evening glow.
styles.furniture: Reclaimed wood and metal, leather (especially cognac), vintage factory and locker pieces, and oversized modular seating for open volumes.
styles.interiorPrompt: industrial loft style with exposed brick and concrete, visible ductwork, black steel-frame windows, reclaimed wood-and-metal furniture and Edison-bulb lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: industrial exterior with brick or board-formed concrete facade, large steel-framed factory windows, metal canopy and minimal hardscaped entry
avoid: cold sterile feel with no warmth
styles.designerNotes: Authentic industrial needs real bones (true lofts, DTLA arts-district conversions); in a standard SoCal house it's a costly illusion (thin-brick veneer, faux beams, steel partitions) that can read fake if overdone. Best deployed selectively, one exposed-brick wall, steel-and-glass office, concrete floors, rather than whole-house. Warmth is the antidote to coldness: layer in reclaimed wood, leather, and warm bulbs so it feels lived-in, not like a parking garage.

styles.name: Bohemian

materials.item: layered kilim, Moroccan, and vintage rugs
warm limewash or saturated accent paint

styles.lighting: Warm and layered: Moroccan pierced-brass lanterns, rattan pendants, string lights, and plenty of candles for a cozy, glowing atmosphere.
styles.furniture: Eclectic and collected: low sofas piled with pillows, floor cushions, poufs, vintage finds, carved wood, and cane, nothing matchy.
styles.interiorPrompt: bohemian style with layered global textiles, abundant plants, rattan and cane furniture, macrame and warm earthy jewel-tone accents
styles.exteriorPrompt: bohemian exterior with warm plaster or stucco, a plant-filled patio, string lights, rattan and textile-draped seating and lush eclectic container gardens
avoid: cluttered mess with no anchor neutrals
one-note beige 'boho' that lost the color
styles.designerNotes: A layered, collected style best built over time from real textiles, vintage finds, and plants, so it's forgiving on budget but easy to cheapen with matchy 'boho in a box' fast-furniture. It suits SoCal creative and rental spaces and pairs naturally with the region's plant culture. The skill is restraint within abundance: anchor the room in warm neutrals so the pattern-mixing reads intentional rather than chaotic.

styles.name: Transitional
signature: blend of traditional forms and clean modern lines
subtle classic millwork without heavy ornament
mix of warm wood and painted finishes

materials.item: simple recessed-panel or clean casing trim
handmade subway or elongated hex tile
brushed nickel, bronze, or brass fixtures

styles.lighting: Balanced and layered: a mix of classic silhouettes in modern finishes, drum-shade pendants, sconces, and recessed light, warm and dimmable.
styles.furniture: Comfortable and tailored: clean-lined sofas with classic proportions, mixed wood and upholstered pieces, and subtle rather than ornate detailing.
styles.interiorPrompt: transitional style blending traditional forms with clean modern lines, a soft neutral palette, shaker cabinetry, oak floors and tailored comfortable furniture
styles.exteriorPrompt: transitional exterior blending classic gables and symmetry with clean modern trim, a neutral palette, mixed siding-and-stone and tidy structured landscaping
styles.designerNotes: The safest, most broadly appealing and resale-friendly style, a middle path that pleases couples split between traditional and modern taste. Mid-tier cost and low risk because the neutral palette and classic-but-simple finishes age slowly. The pitfall is blandness; introduce real texture, one accent color, and a mix of metals and materials so 'timeless' doesn't become 'forgettable builder-beige'.

styles.name: Traditional

materials.item: oak, cherry, or walnut hardwood floors
classic marble or ceramic mosaic tile

styles.lighting: Warm and formal: crystal or brass chandeliers, candle-style sconces, picture lights, and table lamps for a layered, gracious glow.
styles.furniture: Antique and period-inspired: rolled-arm and tufted upholstery, carved wood, wingbacks, formal dining sets, and symmetrical arrangements.
styles.interiorPrompt: traditional style with symmetrical formal balance, rich wood tones, detailed crown molding and wainscoting, classic patterns and period-inspired furniture
styles.exteriorPrompt: traditional exterior with symmetrical colonial or Georgian massing, a formal columned entry, brick or clapboard, shuttered windows and manicured formal landscaping
avoid: heavy dark rooms with no light
styles.designerNotes: Reads formal and established; less common in casual SoCal but sought by clients in stately neighborhoods (Hancock Park, San Marino) and older Colonials. Upper-mid cost driven by custom millwork and quality wood. The risk is feeling dated and dark; keep it fresh with lighter walls, edited (not maximal) patterns, and a few contemporary pieces so it reads classic rather than like a time capsule.

styles.name: Craftsman

materials.item: quarter-sawn white oak trim and built-ins
brick or clinker-brick fireplace and chimney

styles.lighting: Warm and amber: mica-shade lanterns, art-glass pendants, hammered-copper sconces, and leaded-glass fixtures for a soft handcrafted glow.
styles.furniture: Stickley-style mission furniture: straight oak lines, exposed joinery, leather and hammered-copper accents, and hand-thrown pottery.
styles.interiorPrompt: Craftsman style with quarter-sawn oak woodwork and built-ins, exposed beams, wood wainscoting, a handcrafted art-tile fireplace and mica lantern lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: Craftsman bungalow exterior with a low-pitched gable roof, deep overhangs with exposed rafter tails and brackets, tapered porch columns on stone piers and a welcoming front porch
avoid: cool gray palettes that fight the wood
styles.designerNotes: A cornerstone of SoCal housing (Pasadena, Highland Park, and every early-1900s bungalow belt), so with Craftsman the work is usually restoration and reverence, not reinvention; original oak, tile, and built-ins are the home's value. Upper-mid cost because the woodwork is real millwork. The cardinal sin locally is painting over or ripping out original quarter-sawn trim; preserve and restore it, and match new work to the ray-fleck grain.

styles.name: Desert Modern
signature: walls of glass framing desert views
clean mid-century lines with earthy palette

materials.item: large-format stone or concrete floor tile

styles.lighting: Intense filtered daylight softened by breeze-block and deep overhangs; warm brass fixtures, globe pendants, and dramatic landscape uplighting at dusk.
styles.furniture: Mid-century desert: low walnut and leather pieces, sculptural loungers, indoor-outdoor seating, cactus and agave, and bold graphic accents.
styles.interiorPrompt: desert modern Palm Springs style with breeze-block screens, warm plaster walls, walls of glass, walnut cabinetry, terrazzo floors and warm brass mid-century fixtures
styles.exteriorPrompt: desert modern Palm Springs exterior with a butterfly or flat roofline, breeze-block privacy screen, walls of glass, warm plaster, an agave-and-cactus xeriscape and a turquoise pool
avoid: heavy Tuscan or rustic Southwest kitsch
styles.designerNotes: SoCal's own invention (Palm Springs desert modernism), so it fits the climate and light natively and is a strong resale story in the desert and mid-century tracts. Mid-to-upper cost from glass walls and breeze-block, but plaster and landscape do a lot cheaply. The tell of a fake is thin plastic breeze-block and cool-gray palettes; use real concrete block and lean warm and earthy, and let the outdoor connection carry the design.

styles.name: Organic Modern
signature: clean modern lines softened by texture

materials.item: white oak floors and cabinetry, matte
plaster or micro-cement range hood / fireplace

styles.lighting: Warm, soft, and sculptural: plaster and paper pendants, alabaster and ceramic fixtures, and hidden LED washing textured plaster walls.
styles.furniture: Curved and sculptural in natural materials: boucle sofas, rounded oak and travertine tables, organic-form seating, and a few artful ceramics.
styles.interiorPrompt: organic modern style with warm plaster walls, curved sculptural forms, white oak surfaces, travertine accents, boucle and linen textures and soft warm lighting
styles.exteriorPrompt: organic modern exterior with warm plaster volumes, arched openings, natural-wood accents, curved forms, floor-to-ceiling glass and sculptural drought-tolerant landscaping
styles.designerNotes: Currently the most in-demand upper-mid look in SoCal remodels: it's modern's clean lines warmed with plaster, oak, travertine, and curves, sitting between California Casual and Contemporary Luxe. Cost rises with plaster finishes, curved millwork, and stone. The essence is warmth and softness; if it goes gray, hard, or glossy it loses its soul, so keep the palette earthy and let one sculptural plaster or stone moment anchor each room.

styles.name: Art Deco

materials.item: terrazzo or marble floors with inlay
fluted glass, mirror, and stepped detailing

styles.lighting: Glamorous and geometric: fan and sunburst sconces, tiered chandeliers, frosted-glass and stepped-brass fixtures with warm dramatic pooling light.
styles.furniture: Streamlined luxury: curved velvet sofas, lacquered and mirrored casegoods, brass-and-glass tables, and bold symmetrical statement pieces.
styles.interiorPrompt: Art Deco style with bold geometric patterns, chevron parquet floors, lacquered finishes, brass and marble accents, velvet furniture and symmetrical stepped detailing
styles.exteriorPrompt: Art Deco exterior with a stepped symmetrical facade, smooth stucco, vertical fluting, geometric relief detailing, decorative metalwork and streamline-moderne curves
styles.designerNotes: A statement style with real LA heritage (Miracle Mile, Downtown, old Hollywood), best used with confidence and restraint; it's upper-tier cost because lacquer, real marble, brass, and inlay are precision work. Full-house deco can overwhelm, so many clients want a deco-inflected room, a powder bath, bar, or entry, with the drama concentrated. The failure mode is gaudy plastic gold; invest in a few genuine materials and keep the geometry disciplined and symmetrical.

styles.name: French Country
signature: limestone and stone flooring and surrounds
soft muted palette (cream, dusty blue, ochre)

materials.item: carved-stone or plaster range hood / mantel

styles.lighting: Soft and romantic: iron and crystal chandeliers, antique-brass sconces, lantern pendants, and warm candlelight glow.
styles.furniture: Elegant-rustic with patina: cabriole-leg tables, upholstered and cane chairs, armoires, distressed painted pieces, and faded floral linens.
styles.interiorPrompt: French Country style with weathered wood beams, limestone floors, limewashed plaster walls, a soft muted palette, toile textiles and curved patinaed furniture
styles.exteriorPrompt: French Country exterior with limestone or cream stucco walls, a steep hipped roof, shuttered windows, a stone entry, climbing roses and lavender-and-boxwood gardens
styles.designerNotes: A warm, romantic style that suits SoCal wine-country-adjacent and estate homes; it overlaps with Tuscan and Mediterranean but is softer, cooler, and more refined (dusty blue and cream vs. golden ochre). Upper-mid cost from stone and reclaimed timber. The mistake is heavy-handed distressing and matchy 'provincial' suites; aim for genuine patina, edited pattern, and a light muted palette so it feels collected over generations, not staged.

styles.name: Resort / Country Club
signature: rich raised-panel and coffered wood paneling
traditional millwork with a masculine polish

materials.item: walnut or mahogany raised-panel wall paneling
green marble or granite counters/bar tops

styles.lighting: Warm, clubby, and layered: brass banker's lamps with green glass shades, picture lights, tortoiseshell sconces, and low pooled traditional glow.
styles.furniture: Masculine-traditional: tufted leather chesterfields and wingbacks, wood card and billiards tables, brass-nailhead trim, and equestrian and golf memorabilia.
styles.interiorPrompt: resort country-club style with rich wood raised-panel walls, coffered ceilings, tufted leather chesterfield seating, plaid textiles, brass banker's lighting and manicured green views
styles.exteriorPrompt: country-club clubhouse exterior with traditional gabled and dormered massing, stone-and-shingle or brick facade, columned porches, awnings and manicured golf-green landscaping
styles.designerNotes: A polished, masculine-traditional look that reads 'old-money clubhouse'; sought for dens, bars, offices, and estate homes near SoCal golf and equestrian communities. Upper-tier cost because real wood paneling, coffered ceilings, and leather are premium millwork and materials. The line to walk is refined vs. theme-park; keep memorabilia edited and the paneling genuinely well-crafted so it reads like a private club, not a chain steakhouse.

styles.name: Moroccan

materials.item: layered wool and Beni Ourain rugs
poufs, low seating, and jewel-tone textiles

styles.lighting: Atmospheric and patterned: pierced-metal and colored-glass lanterns throw lacy shadows; low warm pooled light and abundant candles.
styles.furniture: Low and layered: carved-wood and inlaid tables, leather poufs, floor cushions, banquette seating, and richly patterned textiles piled deep.
styles.interiorPrompt: Moroccan style with zellige mosaic tilework, carved keyhole arches, tadelakt plaster walls, pierced-brass lanterns and layered jewel-tone rugs and textiles
styles.exteriorPrompt: Moroccan riad exterior with warm plaster walls, keyhole arches, zellige-tiled fountain courtyard, carved-wood doors, brass lanterns and a lush palm-and-citrus garden
avoid: cool gray tones that kill the warmth
styles.designerNotes: A rich, sensory style best deployed in concentrated doses, a tiled fountain courtyard, a tadelakt hammam-style bath, a jewel-box powder room, rather than whole-house, where it can overwhelm. Upper-mid to high cost because real zellige and hand-applied tadelakt are craft trades with lead time and specialist labor. The authenticity test is handmade irregularity; machine-perfect tile and plastic decor read fake, so invest in genuine zellige and real plaster.

styles.name: Wabi-Sabi

materials.item: handmade irregular ceramic and stoneware tile
natural stone with raw or cleft finish
undyed linen, hemp, and raw cotton

styles.lighting: Soft, low, and natural: warm dim ambient light, paper and ceramic fixtures, and an appreciation of shadow and imperfect glow over brightness.
styles.furniture: Handcrafted, worn, and sparse: solid raw-wood pieces with visible age, handmade ceramics, natural textiles, and only what is essential and meaningful.
styles.interiorPrompt: wabi-sabi style with rough hand-troweled plaster walls, raw weathered wood, handmade irregular ceramics, a muted earthy palette and spare intentional composition
styles.exteriorPrompt: wabi-sabi exterior with raw textured plaster, weathered natural wood, patinated metal, an understated form and a restrained naturalistic mossy garden
styles.designerNotes: The most philosophical of the styles: it embraces age, imperfection, and restraint, so it appeals to SoCal clients seeking calm and authenticity over polish. Costs are deceptive, hand-troweled plaster, real reclaimed wood, and artisan ceramics are craft trades, not cheap 'rustic'. The paradox to manage is that intentional imperfection still requires skill and taste; it should feel quietly curated and genuinely aged, never like unfinished or fake-distressed work.
